text.title: accessibility Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for accessibility as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using aria-label to replace missing semantic meaning (e.g., role="button" on a div without keyboard handlers).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: ARIA cannot fix broken semantics. A div with role="button" is still a div; it won't receive keyboard focus by default or respond to Enter/Space. Use native button elements.

failure-mode.description: Div with onclick for interactive elements.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Native button elements receive keyboard focus by default and respond to Enter/Space. Divs require manual keyboard handler code.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Placeholders disappear when typing, leaving users confused. Use explicit labels.

failure-mode.description: Color-only status indicators (red = error, green = success).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Color-blind users cannot distinguish. Add text, icons, or symbols.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Respects user preferences and prevents cognitive overload. Use autoplay="false" and provide pause controls.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Keyboard users need visible focus indicators. Use :focus-visible for better UX than removing outline.

failure-mode.description: Hand-rolled custom components with incorrect ARIA.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: ARIA cannot fix broken semantics. Use native elements (button, a, nav) or Radix/headlessui primitives with built-in accessibility.

failure-mode.description: Generated aria-label strings duplicating text content.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Screen readers read the label twice. Use aria-label only when visual label doesn't exist (icon buttons).

failure-mode.description: Missing focus trap in modals or focus restoration on close.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Screen reader users get lost when focus escapes the modal. Tab must cycle within the modal; Esc returns to opener.
